root_text.sn56.58:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, mahāsamudde udakaṁ parikkhayaṁ pariyādānaṁ gaccheyya ṭhapetvā dve vā tīṇi vā udakaphusitāni.
root_text.sn56.58:1.3: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ—yaṁ vā mahāsamudde udakaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādinnaṁ, yāni dve vā tīṇi vā udakaphusitāni avasiṭṭhānī”ti?
root_text.sn56.58:1.4: “Etadeva, bhante, bahutaraṁ mahāsamudde udakaṁ yadidaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādinnaṁ; appamattakāni dve vā tīṇi vā udakaphusitāni avasiṭṭhāni.
root_text.sn56.58:1.5: Saṅkhampi na upenti, upanidhampi na upenti, kalabhāgampi na upenti mahāsamudde udakaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādinnaṁ upanidhāya dve vā tīṇi vā udakaphusitāni avasiṭṭhānī”ti.

translation_text.sn56.58:1.1: “Mendicants, suppose the water in the ocean dried up and evaporated except for two or three drops.
translation_text.sn56.58:1.3: Which is more: the water in the ocean that has dried up and evaporated, or the two or three drops left?”
translation_text.sn56.58:1.4: “Sir, the water in the ocean that has dried up and evaporated is certainly more. The two or three drops left are tiny.
translation_text.sn56.58:1.5: Compared to the water in the ocean that has dried up and evaporated, it doesn’t count, there’s no comparison, it’s not worth a fraction.”
translation_text.sn56.58:1.6: “In the same way, for a noble disciple …
translation_text.sn56.58:1.7: That’s why you should practice meditation …”

variant_text.sn56.58:1.1: mahāsamudde udakaṁ parikkhayaṁ → mahāsamuddo parikkhayaṁ (bj, sya-all, km)
